Title: SOLICITATION NOTICE FOR UPPER APPLETON WALL REPAIR, APPLETON, OUTAGAMIE COUNTY, WISCONSIN
Awardee: MICHELS CONSTRUCTION, INC. 817 MAIN ST Brownsville WI 53006 USA
Description: ** EFFECTIVE MARCH 2, 2026: The government has posted Amendment No. 0002 hereunder this Notice (please refer to the Attachments Section). ** ** EFFECTIVE FEBRUARY 12, 2026: The government has posted Amendment No. 0001 hereunder this Notice (please refer to the Attachments Section). ** ** Under this Solicitation, the United States Army Corps of Engineers- Chicago District issues Solicitation No. W912P626BA016 for bidding purposes. PROJECT INFORMATION: The scope primarily includes removing the existing material on the upstream face of a stacked masonry stone wall, shotcrete, applying a waterproofing system, and replacing the material to the east and west of the Upper Appleton hydropower plant. The existing wall is roughly 150 years old and has had increased seepage over the past decade. It is founded on bedrock and +/- 20 feet tall. The rehabilitation includes about 365 feet west of the hydropower plant and 140 feet east of the hydropower plant. ACQUISITION INFORMATION: Competition for this acquisition is FULL AND OPEN, UNRESTRICTED TO BUSINESS SIZE. The North American Industry System Classification Code is 237990, with a small business size standard in dollars of $45 Million. The magnitude of cost for construction is estimated between $5,000,000.00 and $10,000,000.00. The requirement is for 439 calendar days. The solicitation IS downloadable from the internet only. The project specification files are PDF files and can be viewed, navigated, or printed using Adobe Acrobat Reader. Responses to this Solicitation (Invitation for Bids) are due by 2:00 p.m. (Central Time) on March 3, 2026. IMPORTANT POLICY: Contractors are REQUIRED to be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) AT THE TIME ITS BID IS SUBMITTED to comply with the annual representations and certifications requirements. Registration can be completed free of charge at www.sam.gov. Contractors must register with sam.gov, prior to registering with the DoD Procurement Integrated Enterprise Environment (PIEE) system. Your firm must register with PIEE in order to access the solicitation and to place a bid in response to the upcoming USACE Solicitation. Use the PIEE System Registration link: ? https://piee.eb.mil and follow the instructions on their website. Request for Solicitation packages made by telephone, fax, or e-mail will not be honored. The Point of Contacts for this acquisition is Ericka Hillard at ericka.d.hillard@usace.army.mil, and Samantha Stiles at samantha.l.stiles@usace.army.mil. Please include Solicitation number W912P626BA016 on all correspondence. **

Title: MDC GUA - Bread - 3rd QTR - FY-26
Sub-Tier: FEDERAL PRISON SYSTEM / BUREAU OF PRISONS
Awardee: HOLSUM DE PUERTO RICO INC Toa Baja PR 00949 USA
Description: March 17, 2026 Post Award Synopsis The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC) Guaynabo, Puerto Rico, announces the award of Purchase Order 15B30626P00000062 under Solicitation 15B30626Q0000006 for Food Items (Bread). The contract has been awarded to Holsum De Puerto Rico. for a total amount of $33,545.00. The procurement was conducted under NAICS 311812, utilizing a Firm?Fixed Price contract structure. This acquisition was solicited on an unrestricted basis, and all responsible sources were permitted to submit a quote. In accordance with FAR 4.1102, all offerors were required to be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) prior to award. Award was made to the offeror whose quote represented the best value to the Government, considering price and past performance. The Government reserved the right to award by item, group, or in aggregate, and exercised this authority in determining the final awardee. Performance under this contract is scheduled to begin on April 1, 2026, through June 30, 2026, at MDC Guaynabo, Highway 28, Intersection of Road 165, Guaynabo, PR 00965?5700. Payments will be made via Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) or Government Purchase Card, as applicable. Description: March 17, 2026 Post Award Synopsis The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC) Guaynabo, Puerto Rico, announces the award of Purchase Order 15B30626P00000061 under Solicitation 15B30626Q0000005 for Food Items (Milk). The contract has been awarded to Vaqueria Tres Monjitas. for a total amount of $78,000.00. The procurement was conducted under NAICS 311812, utilizing a Firm?Fixed Price contract structure. This acquisition was solicited on an unrestricted basis, and all responsible sources were permitted to submit a quote. In accordance with FAR 4.1102, all offerors were required to be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) prior to award. Award was made to the offeror whose quote represented the best value to the Government, considering price and past performance. The Government reserved the right to award by item, group, or in aggregate, and exercised this authority in determining the final awardee. Performance under this contract is scheduled to begin on April 1, 2026, through June 30, 2026, at MDC Guaynabo, Highway 28, Intersection of Road 165, Guaynabo, PR 00965?5700. Faith?Based and Community?Based Organizations were afforded equal opportunity to compete for this requirement in accordance with federal policy. Title: Refrigeration Containers
Awardee: WILLIAMS SCOTSMAN INC Phoenix AZ 85008 USA
Description: This is a notice of intent to exercise FAR 52.217-8, Option to Extend Services in accordance with the terms and conditions in Contract No. W912KC25PA017, awarded to Williams Scotsman Inc., DBA Willscot on August 26, 2025. This is a firm-fixed-price purchase order for the lease extension of five (5) each, 40 Foot Refrigerated Containers with 3 Phase Power- Hi Cube that includes a Remote Monitoring System. The value of this contract action is $25,900.00 increasing the total contract value to $80,780.85. The anticipated period of performance for the extension is April 10, 2026 to July 31, 2026 for the containers located at Fort Indiantown Gap, Annville, Pennsylvania. This will extend services for four months (28-day billing cycles). The contractor will continue to lease the five (5) refrigerated containers to the Government. The contractor will comply with all applicable federal, state, and local regulations, standard industry terms, and provide 24/7 emergency repair services. The Government has determined that the option is the most advantageous method of fulfilling the requirement. This synopsis is for notification purposes only and is not a request for quotes.
